priority_1_blocking.title: Run ImageNet/ResNet-50 experiments
priority_1_blocking.description: ImageNet is a hard project-level requirement (project memory). Run ResNet-50 on ImageNet-1K for {constant, cwd_hard, cosine_schedule, no_wd} × 3 seeds × 90 epochs using DDP on 8 GPUs. This directly tests the Phi Invariance Conjecture at scale and is the NeurIPS reviewer's top demand.
priority_1_blocking.falsification_value: If invariance holds on ImageNet, paper becomes NeurIPS-worthy. If it fails, the boundary condition is identified with precision.

priority_1_blocking.title: Add AdamWN (target-norm axis) to CIFAR benchmark
priority_1_blocking.description: Implement AdamWN: phi_i = max(0, 1 - tau/norm(theta_layer)) where tau = initial layer norm. Add to both AdamW and SGD benchmarks on CIFAR-10/100. This covers the target-norm axis and eliminates the four-axis coverage gap.
priority_1_blocking.falsification_value: If AdamWN shows different behavior (even if not more accurate), it validates CSI and adds a concrete counterexample to naive invariance claims.

priority_2_high.title: Increase seeds to 5+ for key AdamW comparisons
priority_2_high.effort: ~8 GPU-hours, 24 extra runs (2 extra seeds × 3 methods × 2 datasets)
priority_2_high.description: Run 2 additional seeds (e.g., 789, 1234) for {constant, no_wd, cwd_hard} on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 under AdamW. Rerun TOST with delta=0.3% on these 5-seed comparisons. This brings MDE down to ~0.5% and makes the equivalence claim statistically defensible.
priority_2_high.falsification_value: If effects remain <0.3% at N=5, equivalence is confirmed. If some effects emerge in 0.3-0.5% range, the conjecture's precision is improved.

priority_2_high.title: Add CIFAR-100 SGD full results table
priority_2_high.description: The sgd_baseline/cifar100/ directory has full data (7 methods × 3 seeds). Add a Table 5b (or extend Table 5) with all CIFAR-100 SGD results: mean, std, delta, p-value. The SWD significance result (p=0.036) deserves full documentation, not just an inline mention.
priority_2_high.falsification_value: N/A — this is a documentation fix for existing verified data.

priority_2_high.title: Fix AIS threshold framing
priority_2_high.description: Revise Section 3.4 AIS threshold language to distinguish 'alignment signal EXISTS in network' (AIS > 0.2) from 'CWD does NOT exploit more signal than random_mask' (CWD AIS ≈ random_mask AIS). The current framing says AIS > 0.2 means the signal is sufficient for improvement, then says CWD doesn't improve — a logical contradiction. New framing: AIS measures signal strength; CWD's AIS ≈ random_mask's AIS shows CWD doesn't selectively amplify the exploitable component.
priority_2_high.falsification_value: Prevents reviewer confusion and closes a logical gap.

priority_2_high.title: Fix 'second-order perturbation' language
priority_2_high.description: Replace 'second-order perturbation' (Sections 5.4, 6.1) with 'small first-order perturbation proportional to lambda'. The actual bound is O(lambda) in the WD coefficient, not O(lambda^2). Current language implies perturbation theory where corrections are quadratic in the small parameter.
priority_2_high.falsification_value: Prevents technically precise reviewers from flagging an imprecision.

priority_2_high.title: Add SGD weight norm table
priority_2_high.description: The mechanistic argument's strongest evidence (SGD no_wd weight norm 127.1 vs. constant 64.5 = 97% spread) exists in workspace data but has no table. Add Table 6 or an appendix table with SGD weight norms at convergence (7 methods × 2 datasets). Data is in sgd_baseline/*/summary.json fields final_weight_norm.
priority_2_high.falsification_value: Transforms an unverifiable inline claim into documented evidence.

priority_3_medium.title: Run WD warmup ablation (H2 Stability Condition)
priority_3_medium.description: Run CIFAR-10, ResNet-20, constant baseline with WD warmup from 0 to 5e-3 over K ∈ {1, 10, 50, 200, 1000} steps. Measure loss spike magnitude and training loss variance in first 1000 steps. Test whether K < K_critical (1/(lr*wd)) produces instability as predicted by the WD Stability Condition theorem.
priority_3_medium.falsification_value: Provides empirical validation for the paper's only non-trivial theoretical prediction. Even a negative result (no violations found) tightens the conjecture.

priority_3_medium.title: Fix half_lambda BEM implementation
priority_3_medium.description: Re-implement half_lambda with phi=0.5 (phi constant at 0.5 for all parameters) instead of lambda=2.5e-4. Rerun 3 seeds × 2 datasets. This eliminates the BEM implementation bug disclosure footnote entirely and ensures Figure 3's BEM scatter uses pipeline-computed values.
priority_3_medium.falsification_value: Eliminates a reproducibility gap and removes a distracting footnote.

priority_3_medium.title: Test CSI-accuracy correlation under SGD
priority_3_medium.description: Compute Spearman rank correlation between CSI and accuracy under SGD (where methods do differ in accuracy). If CSI correlates with SGD rankings (rho > 0.5) but not AdamW rankings (rho < 0.3), this simultaneously validates CSI as a metric and supports the Phi Invariance Conjecture. Data already exists in sgd_baseline/.
priority_3_medium.falsification_value: Converts CSI from 'uninformative metric' to 'valid metric whose failure to predict under AdamW is itself evidence for invariance.'

priority_3_medium.title: Fix method naming inconsistency
priority_3_medium.description: Standardize: 'cosine-WD' in prose, 'cosine_schedule' in table headers and code. 'no-WD' in prose, 'no_wd' in headers. Apply consistently throughout paper.
priority_3_medium.falsification_value: Cosmetic but eliminates reader friction and grep-based verification issues.

priority_3_medium.title: Add Appendix C (CSI sensitivity analysis) or remove reference to it
priority_3_medium.description: Section 3.4 cites 'Appendix C' for CSI weight sensitivity analysis. The appendix does not exist. Either run the sensitivity analysis (vary w1 in [0.3, 0.5], observe rank correlation stability) and write Appendix C, or remove the reference.
priority_3_medium.falsification_value: Closes a reproducibility gap where a stated verification cannot be done.

priority_4_low.title: Investigate wall-clock time anomaly
priority_4_low.description: The constant-WD method shows 600s for seed 123 but 2400s for seed 42 (4x variation). Determine whether this reflects GPU preemption during experiments, a logging error, or actual training time variation. Document the finding in the reproducibility section.
priority_4_low.falsification_value: If preemption, may have affected training quality of some runs — important for interpreting fine-grained accuracy differences.

priority_4_low.title: Formalize the absorption mechanism as a lemma
priority_4_low.description: The absorption argument in Section 6.1 is qualitatively correct but informal. Formalize as Lemma 1: 'Under AdamW with parameters (beta1, beta2, eps) and weight decay lambda, the per-parameter WD contribution is bounded by ... relative to the gradient update, for any Phi modulator with phi_i in [0,1].' This provides a quantitative bound that makes the conjecture provable under assumptions about the gradient distribution.
priority_4_low.falsification_value: Elevates the paper from 'empirical conjecture' to 'theorem + empirical validation.' Changes the paper's theoretical status significantly.
